reasoning: 386 hanabi festivals from Wikipedia/Wikidata returned — directly addresses intent. RULE C: most items lack prefecture, dates, and coordinates; agent cannot filter by region or season. Lang=en returned but names remain Japanese-only. Coverage is wide but planning utility is low without dates or locations.
missing_data: Dates/months, English event names, coordinates for most items
improvement_hint: Add date_month and location fields to hanabi entries; sort by prominence so canonical festivals (Sumida, Nagaoka, Ōmagari) surface first.

reasoning: Same 386-item hanabi list as L3-01 but lang=ja correctly matched. Intent is served but items lack dates and most lack coordinates, limiting trip-planning use. Single root cause per RULE B: missing structured date/location data drives both practicality and specificity gaps.
missing_data: Dates, coordinates for most items, prefecture for many
improvement_hint: Inject date_start/date_end from Wikidata P585/P580 for hanabi QIDs; rank canonical festivals to top.

reasoning: RULE C: Hokkaido filter returned zero local festivals with a helpful advisory noting Sapporo Yuki Matsuri is not yet ingested. The alternative hint (search_area with '雪まつり') is useful. Query asks broadly 'Japan' but tool was called with Hokkaido; also the query_intent shows a 'kaido' concept mismatch — likely intent classification noise.
missing_data: Sapporo Snow Festival and other major snow festivals (Yokote Kamakura, Towada Winter Festival)
improvement_hint: Ingest major snow festivals as Schema.org Event records; fix intent classifier to not inject 'kaido' concept for snow-festival queries.

reasoning: Query is about rural village life experiences (田舎暮らし体験). Tool returned get_japan_heritage with national treasures (Nagoya Castle, Jo-an teahouse, Sannai-Maruyama) — culturally rich but not rurally experiential. RULE A applied: data is authoritative heritage data but addresses wrong intent. Per RULE B worst dim is intent_understanding.
missing_data: Rural homestay / satoyama experiences, farmstay programs, WWOOF-type agritourism venues
improvement_hint: Route '田舎暮らし' queries to search_hybrid with kinds filter for rural/agri-tourism rather than get_japan_heritage; add satoyama and noson_taiken kinds.

reasoning: RULE D: query is in Korean (ko) but items have only name_ja and name_en fields — no Korean names returned despite lang=ko. Per RULE B this is a single constraint failure (language). Data itself is strong: 140 results include sakura_meisho_100 spots and heritage sites. Kenroku-en and Himeji Castle visible.
missing_data: Korean (ko) translations for attraction names and descriptions
improvement_hint: Ensure name_ko field is populated from multilingual index and returned when lang=ko is requested.

reasoning: Query asks for quiet meditation temple, not touristy. Response returns 修行-related spots including Shugyō-ji (training temple), a Rinzai-ji 修行体験 event in Shizuoka, and a ninja training experience — the last is clearly off-intent. No crowd_band filtering applied. 修行体験 event (Rinzai-ji, ¥3,000) is genuinely useful. RULE B: reasoning failure as quiet/non-touristy constraint not filtered.
missing_data: Zazen / shukubo venues, Eiheiji, Koyasan retreats, crowd_band=low temples
improvement_hint: Filter results by crowd_band=low when query contains quiet/non-touristy signals; add shukubo and zazen_experience kinds.

reasoning: Excellent not_available advisory block with scientifically accurate aurora explanation, then surfaces dark_sky/observatory alternatives (Sendai Astronomical Observatory, Kushiro Wetland, Akashi Planetarium). Lang=es constraint: response metadata and advisory text are in en/ja, not es — RULE D: -1 on constraint_handling. Otherwise strong handling of impossible query.
missing_data: Spanish-language advisory text
improvement_hint: Translate not_available reason_en/reason_ja into the requested lang; add reason_{lang} field for supported languages.

reasoning: Query asks for 100-year-old houses (古民家) to stay in. Tool returned get_hotels(Gifu) which returns onsen ryokan, a hostel, and an Airbnb — none tagged as kominka/machiya. Gifu is a reasonable guess (has kominka), but the data type mismatch is fundamental. RULE A: per RULE B, worst impact is on intent_understanding (the core ask — architectural heritage accommodation — is absent).
missing_data: Kominka-type accommodations, machiya stays; Shirakawa-go farmhouse lodgings specifically
improvement_hint: Add kominka/machiya lodging_type tags to hotel records; expose filter parameter for lodging_type.

reasoning: Query is in Thai asking for non-touristy spots. Tool called search_area(q='桜', lang='th'). Returns 215 matches of sakura spots. Two issues: (1) lang=th returns no Thai translations per RULE D constraint failure; (2) query intent 'non-touristy' is not filtered — Kenroku-en and Himeji Castle appear which are iconic tourist spots. RULE B: constraint failure is primary.
missing_data: Thai translations; low-crowd sakura alternatives like Minobusan, Takato, or lesser-known spots
improvement_hint: Populate name_th translations; apply crowd_band=low preference when query contains non-tourist signals.

reasoning: Query: dramatic landscapes for photography. Response returns 53 results with '絶景' keyword. Top results are editorial articles/feature pages (Ehime tourism route, Niigata zekkei collection) rather than specific named viewpoints. Authoritative data present but buried under article-type nav-chrome spots. RULE C: ranking failure per RULE B — individual named viewpoints like Tottori Dunes, Takeda Castle, Naruto whirlpools should lead.
missing_data: Specific named dramatic landscape viewpoints with coordinates; popular photography spots
improvement_hint: Demote article/feature-page spots in ranking; boost type=attraction with named coordinates for 絶景 queries.

reasoning: Query in Arabic: outdoor hot spring baths not in hotels. Response from search_area('露天風呂') has no Arabic translations (RULE D). Results include some relevant outdoor onsen scrape spots (Hohsenboh, Haraikawa) but also indoor bath facilities. Constraint 'not in hotels' not filtered. Per RULE B the language constraint failure is primary.
missing_data: Arabic translations; filtering for outdoor/nature-only rotenburo (no hotel designation)
improvement_hint: Add Arabic to multilingual index; filter out hotel-type results when query explicitly requests non-hotel baths.

reasoning: Query: swim with wild dolphins. Top result is イルカパーク (captive dolphin park, Iki) — opposite of 'wild'. Other results include an aquarium (Notojima), Kyoto cultural ambassador page (irrelevant), and a fish market. The one genuinely relevant result is partially buried. RULE B: reasoning failure — captive vs. wild distinction not handled.
missing_data: Mikurajima wild dolphin swimming, Amami Oshima dolphin watching, Ogasawara wild dolphin spots
improvement_hint: Add wild_dolphin_watching kind; filter out aquarium/captive results when query contains 'wild' or similar constraint; boost marine wildlife spots.

reasoning: Query: traditional architecture untouched by modernization — maps perfectly to 重伝建 (preservation districts). Tool returned get_japan_heritage which returns 日本遺産 narratives and Wikidata heritage entities. Nagoya Castle, Sannai-Maruyama are historic but not 'untouched streetscapes'. RULE C: authoritative but scope gap — should have routed to search_area or a preservation_district filter.
missing_data: 重要伝統的建造物群保存地区 (Shirakawa-go, Gion Higashiyama, Tsumago, Uchiko)
improvement_hint: Add routing logic so 'traditional architecture / streetscape' queries prioritize preservation_district kind results; or expose get_preservation_districts tool.

reasoning: Query in Vietnamese about Ainu culture in Hokkaido. Response returns 129 results with strong top-tier hits: Ainu Museum (Porotokotan/Upopoy), National Ainu Museum, Nibutani Ainu Culture Museum, Hokkaido Ainu Center, Kawamura Kaneto Ainu Museum — all Hokkaido. Excellent topical coverage. RULE D: lang=vi returns only name_ja/name_en, no Vietnamese names — single constraint failure per RULE B.
missing_data: Vietnamese (vi) translations of names and descriptions
improvement_hint: Populate name_vi from multilingual index for high-prominence Ainu cultural sites; ensure vi lang is supported in get_description.

reasoning: Query: cliff-carved Buddhist statues (磨崖仏). Response perfectly surfaces Usuki Stone Buddhas (National Treasure, prominence 0.9), Ōya-dera (prominence 0.9), Komosaka Magaibutsu, Kiyomizu Magaibutsu, and others across multiple prefectures. All authoritative Wikidata + Bunka-cho sources. Heritage designations correct. One caveat: some lack English names — minor expression gap.
missing_data: English names for several entries (狛坂磨崖仏, 大岩弘法院磨崖仏)
improvement_hint: Backfill English names for lesser-known magaibutsu from Wikidata label/sitelink; add description_en for these entries.

reasoning: Query: day-trip volcano hikes from Tokyo. Response returns volcanoes nationwide — first result is Mount Iwate (Iwate Prefecture, ~4h from Tokyo), then Fuji (feasible), Lake Towada (Aomori, ~6h), Mount Unzen (Nagasaki, ~12h). No distance-from-Tokyo filtering applied. Per RULE B: single constraint failure (geographic proximity) is worst dim. Fuji is actually correct but buried at #2.
missing_data: Day-trip filtering for Tokyo proximity; Fuji, Hakone, Izu volcanoes should lead
improvement_hint: Add geo-proximity ranking when query contains 'from [city]' pattern; boost Fuji/Hakone/Izu for Tokyo-origin queries.

reasoning: Query in Indonesian: eat freshly made tofu at a temple. Top result is MAFF GI record for Iwateyama Kori-tofu (freeze-dried, not fresh temple tofu). Second result is a detailed piece on Ikishima tofu production. The 金剛山山の豆腐まつまさ result (tofu shop at Kongōsan trailhead) is relevant but not a temple. No temple (寺) + tofu combination surfaced. RULE B: reasoning failure — 'at a temple' constraint not synthesized.
missing_data: Nanzenji tofu restaurants (Kyoto), Koyasan shojin-ryori tofu, temple tofu experiences
improvement_hint: For compound queries (food + venue type), route through hybrid search combining ingredient term + venue kind filter; add shojin_ryori kind.

reasoning: Query: fishing villages frozen in time. Top result is a Shizuoka sakura-ebi restaurant feature (not a village). Second is Itoman fishing port (active, modern). Relevant result — Tainoura (鯛の浦, fishing_village kind, port_town) — is in the heritage data. Most results describe active fishing ports or food articles, not preserved historic villages. RULE B: reasoning failure, should surface fishing_village kind with preservation tags.
missing_data: Ine (伊根) funaya boathouses, Kujukuri, Choshi historic fishing district, wajima port area
improvement_hint: Add fishing_village preservation_district cross-kind filter; boost Wikidata heritage fishing_village kinds over scrape articles about fish markets.

reasoning: Query: learn washi paper making. Response returns get_local_specialty(craft) with 231 METI traditional crafts. Top results are Nibutani Attus textile and Oitama Tsumugi silk — no washi in top visible items. Coverage is comprehensive (231 crafts) but washi entries (Echizen washi, Mino washi, Ogawa washi) are not surfaced in first visible items. RULE C: data present but ranking gap.
missing_data: Washi paper-making workshop/experience venues; Echizen washi, Mino washi UNESCO entries surfaced higher
improvement_hint: Add experience-type filtering or routing for craft+workshop queries; rank UNESCO-designated crafts (Washi inscribed 2014) higher for paper-making queries.

reasoning: Query: period-drama-like scenery. search_area('宿場') returns 104 hits led by Tsumago-juku (must_see, IPDT designation), Narai-juku, Seki-juku — all are Edo-period post towns with preservation designations. Municipal scrape enriches with narrative descriptions of Nakasendo post towns. Excellent intent match. Minor: some attractions lack English names for international agent use.
missing_data: Gion Higashiyama, Kyoto Ninenzaka also match intent but not in top results
improvement_hint: Add cross-kind boost for shukuba + preservation_district + machiya combinations; ensure film-set/jidaigeki adjacent terms map to this cluster.

reasoning: Query: uncrowded beaches. Response returns 115 beach results. Top results include Naminoue Beach (Okinawa, crowd_band=medium), Sunayama Beach, Tropical Beach — all medium or low crowd. The crowd constraint 'not crowded' is partially honored by crowd_band=low entries appearing, but medium entries still dominate top positions. No negative-constraint filtering applied for '穴場'. RULE B: constraint failure is primary.
missing_data: Crowd_band=low beach filter; remote beaches (Amami, Ogasawara, Tsushima)
improvement_hint: Apply crowd_band=low preference when query contains 穴場 / 'not crowded' / 'hidden' signals; boost remote islands.

reasoning: Query: old-school izakaya with locals. search_area('横丁') returns 74 results with yokocho-tagged venues. First result is '横丁公園' (a park tagged yokocho — not an izakaya alley). Second is a Saga tourism page in Chinese. Haikara't Yokocho (Yamaguchi), Naniwa Kuishimbo Yokocho appear but are theme parks/food courts, not authentic local izakaya alleys. Famous alleys like Yurakucho, Nonbei Yokocho absent. RULE B: ranking failure.
missing_data: Yurakucho Yakitori Alley, Shinjuku Omoide Yokocho, Nishi-Ogikubo alley spots
improvement_hint: Boost yokocho kind entries with municipal_scrape food/nightlife context over park entries; add izakaya_alley sub-kind.

reasoning: Query in German: travel rural Japan by train. Response surfaces Sagano Scenic Line, Ōigawa Main Line (SL railway), Ueda Dentetsu Bessho Line narrative, Oga Line, Ōu Main Line — all authentic local railways. Good topical match. RULE D: lang=de but no German translations in names or descriptions — single constraint failure per RULE B. Otherwise data quality is strong.
missing_data: German (de) translations; Iiyama Line, Tadami Line, other scenic rural railways
improvement_hint: Populate name_de / description_de for railway entries; add de to supported languages in multilingual index.

reasoning: Query: firefly viewing in summer. First result is ホタルイカ (firefly squid, different species) — a Natural Monument at Toyama Bay, not a firefly (ホタル = Lampyridae). Though correctly tagged and a legitimate hit for the kanji 蛍 subset, agent must disambiguate. Subsequent results (美郷のホタル, 豊田ホタルの里ミュージアム) are on-target. Ranking places squid above fireflies. RULE B: ranking failure.
missing_data: Specific firefly season dates (June peak); Motosu Lake, Okunikko firefly viewing spots
improvement_hint: Distinguish ホタルイカ (squid) from ホタル (firefly) in kinds tagging; route 'firefly viewing' queries to firefly-specific kind rather than text match on kanji alone.

reasoning: Query in Russian: see cranes in winter. Top result is Izumi crane migration grounds (Kagoshima, 10,000 cranes, Oct–Mar) with correct description and heritage designations — excellent match. Kushiro Wetland (Hokkaido red-crowned cranes) also appears. However many results are municipalities named 鶴 (Tsuru) which are noise — Tsuruta-cho, Tsurui-mura, Yokohama Tsurumi Ward. RULE D: lang=ru returns no Russian translations — constraint failure per RULE B.
missing_data: Russian translations; Tsurui/Akan area crane spotting info; noise municipalities should be filtered
improvement_hint: Populate name_ru for key wildlife sites; improve disambiguation to de-prioritize municipality name matches when attraction-kind matches exist for the same query.
